member.name: Sir Gavin Williamson
member.fullTitle: Rt Hon Sir Gavin Williamson MP
member.role: MP for Stone, Great Wyrley and Penkridge

addresses: Haling Dene Centre, Cannock Road, Penkridge, Stafford, Staffordshire, ST19 5DT

member.synopsis: The Rt Hon Sir Gavin Williamson is the Conservative MP for Stone, Great Wyrley and Penkridge , and has been an MP continually since 6 May 2010.

officialSourceNotes: Current member identity, party, constituency, thumbnail, contact information, synopsis, written questions, and registered interests are generated from the official UK Parliament Members API.
Recent Commons division positions are generated from the official UK Parliament Commons Votes API.
External official links are retained as citations; TOR Phi profile and record navigation remains internal.

writtenQuestions.heading: Offences against Children: Prisoners' Release
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Justice, how many victims of the rapists and child groomers released early under the scheme were notified in advance through the Victim Contact Scheme in the constituency of Stone, Great Wyrley and Penkridge.
writtenQuestions.answerText: This Government inherited a prison system on the brink of collapse, and we are fixing this crisis. Through the introduction of the landmark Sentencing Act 2026, we are ensuring that courts retain the ability to impose custodial sentences on serious and da...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Justice

writtenQuestions.heading: Sexual Offences: Prisoners' Release
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Justice, which offences under the Sexual Offences Act 2003 are excluded from eligibility for the early release scheme.
writtenQuestions.answerText: I extend my sincere sympathy to all victims of these appalling crimes. This Government inherited a prisons system days from collapse. We had no choice but to take decisive action to stop our prisons overflowing and keep the public safe. Our landmark Sente...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Justice

writtenQuestions.answerText: I extend my sincere sympathy to all victims of these appalling crimes. This Government inherited a prisons system days from collapse. We had no choice but to take decisive action to stop our prisons overflowing and keep the public safe. Our landmark Sente...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Justice

writtenQuestions.heading: Freeview Service
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Culture, Media and Sport, what estimate her Department has made of the cost to households on fixed incomes of switching from Freeview to broadband television.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Parliament has secured the future of freeview, also known as digital terrestrial television (DTT), until at least 2034 and no decision has been made about what happens beyond this. On 23 June the Government published a Green Paper, Watch this Space: A new...

writtenQuestions.heading: Police and Crime Commissioners

writtenQuestions.heading: Farms: Tenancy Agreements
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, if she will make an assessment of the potential merits of increasing the length of short term Farm Business Tenancies beyond two years.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government recognises the benefits that longer term tenancies can provide in delivering food security and environmental goals. In the recently published Land Use Framework the Government committed to working with landowners, including members of the N...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

writtenQuestions.heading: Bovine Tuberculosis: Vaccination
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, what steps she is taking to accelerate the process of TB vaccinations for cows.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The CattleBCG vaccine, when coupled with the new Detect Infected amongst Vaccinated Animals (DIVA) skin test, represents a significant advancement in bovine TB control for cattle herds. Field trials began in June 2021, with the final planned phase now und...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

writtenQuestions.heading: Health Services: Asylum
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what estimate his Department has made of the cost to the NHS of providing healthcare to asylum seekers.
writtenQuestions.answerText: DHSC and NHSE do not hold the information requested. The overall management of people seeking asylum is a matter for the Home Office.The NHS is a residency-based system; this means that people who do not live here on a lawful, settled basis must contribut...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Coroners: Perinatal Mortality
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Justice, whether responsibility for reporting to Parliament on coronial investigations of stillbirths following the 2019 consultation rests with his Department.
writtenQuestions.answerText: I would like to extend my deepest sympathies to all parents bereaved by baby loss. Bereavement is never easy, but the loss of a child is unimaginable, and we recognise the profound and lifelong impact this has on parents and families.As I set out in my an...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Justice

writtenQuestions.heading: Jobcentres: Closures
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Work and Pensions, what criteria will be taken into consideration in decisions on job centre closures.
writtenQuestions.answerText: When considering moving colleagues and services to an alternative location the Department considers a wide range of factors and evidence. These include the impact on customers, business needs, local labour market conditions, and the ability to maintain a ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Work and Pensions
